behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is a module handoff: summary write path must query/join stored per-dimension findings for the same round before allowing PASS. A unit test of only summary-text logic would miss this cross-module state check. No behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, and correctness does not depend on LLM output content, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Wire summary-service + findings-repository (in-process DB/fixture). Seed one round with at least one FAIL dimension finding, attempt to persist PASS summary, assert write is rejected and reason references stored FAIL for that round.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Bug is specifically trust-boundary between gate computation and persisted findings vs manual summary text. Need wired modules (gate + storage + summary input) to catch regression. Text does not require real infra; it also does not require model judgment quality, so higher levels are not required.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored findings containing a FAIL while providing hand-written summary text claiming all PASS. Execute stop-gate verdict computation through service boundary and assert verdict is non-PASS based on stored findings, not summary text.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Two-module interaction is the failure boundary: override authorization check plus audit-record emission/persistence. Unit-only validation of flag parsing would miss missing audit write. Behavior text does not require real OS/log backend/network, and does not depend on LLM output quality, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke summary-finalization path twice: (1) manual override text without flag -> reject; (2) same with explicit override flag -> allow only if warning/audit record is persisted with actor, round, and timestamp fields.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Failure is ordering/state propagation across local modules: fix-commit metadata and review-round metadata must be compared before PASS. A unit test of comparator logic alone may miss wrong data source wiring. System rejected because behavior text does not require real git subprocess/OS semantics; Agentic/Workflow rejected because requirement is temporal linkage, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Set up commit metadata service + review-round store + gate service. Create latest fix commit C2 and an older round tied to C1; assert PASS blocked. Create new round tied to C2 (or newer) and assert PASS can proceed.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets orchestration/state gating: whether a post-fix review round execution event exists for the updated diff. This can be caught by wiring loop controller + round-state store + agent-run adapter with deterministic stubbed run completion. Agentic rejected by behavior text because assertion is about run freshness/existence, not correctness of model-generated findings; Workflow rejected because this behavior does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate live loop with updated diff D2. Case A: only pre-fix round exists -> finalize PASS must be blocked. Case B: adapter records fresh round executed on D2 -> PASS finalization may proceed (subject to other gates).

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is gate logic consuming two persisted states: rounds-remaining/exhausted and unresolved MUST-FIX findings. Needs module wiring between round-tracker and findings store. No real OS/network/subprocess or model-output quality requirement is stated, so System/Agentic/Workflow are unnecessary.
behaviors.test_description: Seed exhausted round counter and stored unresolved MUST-FIX findings. Run final outcome computation and assert result is escalation, explicitly not PASS.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly specifies a live multi-round review workflow and exhaustion semantics across rounds; failure appears in sequenced pipeline state transitions, not a single boundary. Multiple review-agent rounds are part of the requirement, so minimum boundary is full workflow execution. Integration/Agentic alone are disqualified by behavior text 'live multi-round review workflow' and 'exhausted rounds' requiring end-to-end sequential progression.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end review pipeline over multiple rounds on evolving diffs: produce unresolved MUST-FIX findings, consume all allowed rounds, then attempt finalization. Assert PASS remains blocked and escalation event is emitted with round-exhausted context.
